title: Positive strand RNA viruses differ in the constraints they place on the folding of their negative strand  - data files and images
author: Morgan Herod
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Positive strand RNA virus replication requires the production of a negative strand RNA intermediate which is used as a template for more positive strand synthesis. Data in this repository is from a study submitted for publication which uses ribozymes as a tool to probe the folding of this negative strand RNA intermediate in a variety of different positive strand RNA viruses.
steps_raw: Positive strand RNA virus replication requires the production of a negative strand RNA intermediate which is used as a template for more positive strand synthesis. Data in this repository is from a study submitted for publication which uses ribozymes as a tool to probe the folding of this negative strand RNA intermediate in a variety of different positive strand RNA viruses.
